      Refereed Journals

      1. Ruben Minasian, Ioannis Papadimitriou, Piljin Yi, Anomalies and Supersymmetry, [arXiv:2104.13391].

      2. Pankaj Chaturvedi, Ioannis Papadimitriou, Wei Song, Boyang Yu, $AdS_3$ gravity and the complex SYK models, [arXiv:2011.10001], JHEP05(2021)142.

      3. George Katsianis, Ioannis Papadimitriou, Kostas Skenderis, Marika Taylor, Supersymmetry anomaly in the superconformal Wess-Zumino model, [arXiv:2011.09506], JHEP02(2021)209.

      4. Giorgos Anastasiou, Olivera Miskovic, Rodrigo Olea, Ioannis Papadimitriou, Counterterms, Kounterterms, and the variational problem in AdS gravity, [arXiv:2003.06425], JHEP08(2020)061.

      5. Ioannis Papadimitriou, Supersymmetry anomalies in new minimal supergravity, [arXiv:1904.00347], JHEP09(2019)039.

      6. Ioannis Papadimitriou, Supersymmetry anomalies in $N = 1$ conformal supergravity, [arXiv:1902.06717], JHEP04(2019)040.

      7. George Katsianis, Ioannis Papadimitriou, Kostas Skenderis, Marika Taylor, Anomalous Supersymmetry, [arXiv:1902.06715], Phys. Rev. Lett. 122, 231602 (2019).

      8. Alejandra Castro, Finn Larsen, Ioannis Papadimitriou, 5D Rotating Black Holes and the $nAdS_2/nCFT_1$ Correspondence, [arXiv:1807.06988], JHEP10(2018)042.

      9. Sera Cremonini, Mirjam Cvetič, Ioannis Papadimitriou, Thermoelectric DC conductivities in hyperscaling violating Lifshitz theories, [arXiv:1801.04284], JHEP04(2018)099.

      10. Alejandro Cabo-Bizet, Uri Kol, Leopoldo A. Pando Zayas, Ioannis Papadimitriou, Vimal Rathee, Entropy functional and the holographic attractor mechanism, [arXiv:1712.01849], JHEP05(2018)155.

      11. Ioannis Papadimitriou, Supercurrent anomalies in 4d SCFTs, [arXiv:1703.04299], JHEP07(2017)038.

      12. Marco M. Caldarelli, Ariana Christodoulou, Ioannis Papadimitriou, Kostas Skenderis, Phases of planar AdS black holes with axionic charge, [arXiv:1612.07214], JHEP04(2017)001.

      13. Johanna Erdmenger, Carlos Hoyos, Andy O’Bannon, Ioannis Papadimitriou, Jonas Probst, Jackson M. S. Wu, Two-point Functions in a Holographic Kondo Model, [arXiv:1612.02005], JHEP03(2017)039.

      14. Johanna Erdmenger, Carlos Hoyos, Andy O’Bannon, Ioannis Papadimitriou, Jonas Probst, Jackson M. S. Wu, Holographic Kondo and Fano Resonances, [arXiv:1611.09368], Phys. Rev. D 96, 021901 (2017).

      15. Mirjam Cvetič, Ioannis Papadimitriou, $AdS_2$ Holographic Dictionary, [arXiv:1608.07018], JHEP12(2016)008.

      16. Maximilian Attems, Jorge Casalderrey-Solana, David Mateos, Ioannis Papadimitriou, Daniel Santos-Oliván, Carlos F. Sopuerta, Miquel Triana, Miguel Zilhão, Thermodynamics, transport and relaxation in non-conformal theories, [arXiv:1603.01254], JHEP10(2016)155.

      17. Ok Song An, Mirjam Cvetič, Ioannis Papadimitriou, Black hole thermodynamics from a variational principle: Asymptotically conical backgrounds, [arXiv:1602.01508], JHEP03(2016)086.

      18. Andy O’Bannon, Ioannis Papadimitriou, Jonas Probst, Ioannis Papadimitriou, A holographic two-impurity Kondo model, [arXiv:1510.08123], JHEP01(2016)103.

      19. Matteo Bertolini, Daniele Musso, Ioannis Papadimitriou, Himanshu Raj, A goldstino at the bottom of the cascade, [arXiv:1509.03594], JHEP11(2015)184.

      20. Jonathan Lindgren, Ioannis Papadimitriou, Anastasios Taliotis, Joris Vanhoof, Holographic Hall conductivities from dyonic backgrounds, [arXiv:1505.04131], JHEP07(2015)094.

      21. Wissam Chemissany, Ioannis Papadimitriou, Lifshitz holography: The whole shebang, [arXiv:1408.0795], JHEP01(2015)052.

      22. Wissam Chemissany, Ioannis Papadimitriou, Generalized dilatation operator method for non-relativistic holography, [arXiv:1405.3965], Phys. Lett. B 737 (2014) 272–276.

      23. Ioannis Papadimitriou, Anastasios Taliotis, Riccati equations for holographic 2-point functions, [arXiv:1312.7876], JHEP04(2014)194.

      24. João Aparício, Daniel Grumiller, Esperanza Lopez, Ioannis Papadimitriou, Stefan Stricker, Bootstrapping gravity solutions, [arXiv:1212.3609], JHEP05(2013)128.

      25. Ioannis Papadimitriou, Holographic Renormalization of general dilaton-axion gravity, [arXiv:1106.4826], JHEP08(2011)119.

      26. Ioannis Papadimitriou, Holographic renormalization as a canonical transformation, [arXiv:1007.4592], JHEP11(2010)014.

      27. Jerome Gaillard, Dario Martelli, Carlos Núñez, Ioannis Papadimitriou, The warped, resolved, deformed conifold gets flavoured, [arXiv:1004.4638], Nucl. Phys. B 843 (2011) 1.

      28. Carlos Núñez, Ioannis Papadimitriou, Maurizio Piai, Walking Dynamics from String Duals, [arXiv:0812.3655], Int. J. Mod. Phys. A25 (2010) 2837-2865.

      29. Carlos Hoyos-Badajoz, Carlos Núñez, Ioannis Papadimitriou, Comments on the String dual to $N=1$ SQCD, [arXiv:0807.3039], Phys. Rev. D78 (2008) 086005.

      30. Ioannis Papadimitriou, Multi-Trace Deformations in AdS/CFT: Exploring the Vacuum Structure of the Deformed CFT, [arXiv:hep-th/0703152], JHEP05(2007)075.

      31. Sebastian de Haro, Ioannis Papadimitriou, Anastasios C. Petkou, Conformally Coupled Scalars, Instantons and Vacuum Instability in $AdS_4$, [arXiv:hep-th/0611315], Phys. Rev. Lett. 98 (2007) 231601.

      32. Ioannis Papadimitriou, Non-Supersymmetric Membrane Flows from Fake Supergravity and Multi-Trace Deformations, [arXiv:hep-th/0606038], JHEP02(2007)008.

      33. Ioannis Papadimitriou, Kostas Skenderis, Thermodynamics of asymptotically locally AdS spacetimes, [arXiv:hep-th/0505190], JHEP08(2005)004.

      34. Ioannis Papadimitriou, Kostas Skenderis, Correlation functions in holographic RG flows, [arXiv:hep-th/0407071], JHEP10(2004)075.

      35. Mirjam Cvetič, Ioannis Papadimitriou, More supersymmetric standard - like models from intersecting D6-branes on type IIA orientifolds, [arXiv:hep-th/0303197], Phys. Rev. D67 (2003) 126006.

      36. Mirjam Cvetič, Ioannis Papadimitriou, Conformal field theory couplings for intersecting D-branes on orientifolds, [arXiv:hep-th/0303083], Phys. Rev. D68 (2003) 046001.

      37. Mirjam Cvetič, Ioannis Papadimitriou, Gary Shiu, Supersymmetric three family SU(5) grand unified models from type IIA orientifolds with intersecting D6-branes, [arXiv:hep-th/0212177], Nucl. Phys. B659 (2003) 193-223.

      38. Richard C. Thompson, John Papadimitriou, Simple model for the laser cooling of an ion in a Penning trap, J. Phys. B: At. Mol. Opt. Phys. 33 (2000) 3393-3405.

      Refereed Conference Proceedings

      1. Ioannis Papadimitriou, Lectures on Holographic Renormalization, Proceedings of the School on Theoretical Frontiers in Black Holes and Cosmology, Natal, Brazil, Jun. 8 - 19, 2015, Springer Proc. Phys. 176 (2016) 131-181.

      2. Ioannis Papadimitriou, Hyperscaling violating Lifshitz holography, [arXiv:1411.0312], Proceedings of the 37th International Conference on High Energy Physics, ICHEP 2014, Valencia, Spain, Jul. 2 - 9, 2014, Nucl. and Particle Phys. Proceedings, Vol. 273-275, pp. 1487-1493.

      3. Johanna Erdmenger, Kazuo Ghoroku, Rene Meyer, Ioannis Papadimitriou, Holographic Cosmological Backgrounds, Wilson Loop (De)confinement and Dilaton Singularities, [arXiv:1205.0677], Proceedings of the 17th European Workshop on String Theory 2011, Padua, Italy, Sep. 5 - 9, 2011, Fortsch.Phys. 60 (2012) 991-997.

      4. Ioannis Papadimitriou, Kostas Skenderis, AdS/CFT correspondence and geometry, [arXiv:hep-th/0404176], Proceedings of the 73rd Meeting between Physicists and Mathematicians: ‘AdS/CFT correspondence’, Strasbourg, Sep. 11 - 13, 2003, IRMA Lectures in Mathematics and Theoretical Physics Vol. 8, AdS/CFT Correspondence: Einstein Metrics and Their Conformal Boundaries, ISBN 978-3-03719-013-5.

      5. Ioannis Papadimitriou, Holographic renormalization made simple: An example, Proceedings of the International School of Subnuclear Physics: 41st Course: From Quarks to Black Holes: Progress in Understanding the Logic of Nature, Erice, Sicily, Italy, Aug. 29 - Sep. 7, 2003.

      Ph.D. Thesis

      Ioannis Papadimitriou, Aspects of the Gauge/Gravity Duality and Holography, University of Amsterdam (2005), ISBN 9064642567.

      Master Thesis

      Ioannis Papadimitriou, Optimization of order parameters in lattice SU(2) gauge theory using the linear delta expansion, Imperial College London (2000).
